title: Sonnets From The Portuguese XXV
content:
A heavy heart, Belov�d, have I borne
    From year to year until I saw thy face,
    And sorrow after sorrow took the place
    Of all those natural joys as lightly worn
    As the stringed pearls, each lifted in its turn
    By a beating heart at dance-time. Hopes apace
    Were changed to long despairs, till God�s own grace
    Could scarcely lift above the world forlorn
    My heavy heart. Then thou didst bid me bring
    And let it drop adown thy calmly great
    Deep being! Fast it sinketh, as a thing
    Which its own nature does precipitate,
    While thine doth close above it, mediating
    Betwixt the stars and the unaccomplished fate.
meaning: About: Elizabeth Barrett Browning was one of the most respected poets of the Victorian era.
